18.reason: This should be else if to avoid multiple conditions being checked when the first if is true. Without else if, after the first if increments i, the second if may access out-of-bounds or incorrect characters.

30.reason: [This `else` only pairs with the last `if`, not all three. If any of the first two `if`s match but the third doesn't, it will incorrectly print '-'. Also, when a valid expansion occurs, the code increments `i` but then continues to check subsequent `if`s or the `else`, which can lead to double output or incorrect behavior.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This else only attaches to the last if, so if the first or second if is true, this else will not execute, but if the third if is false, it will print the '-' even when expansion was already done by the first or second if.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.1 , reason:  [This else is attached only to the third if; if the first or second if was true, the else will still execute (since third if is false) and print the dash incorrectly]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Due to missing 'else if' above, this 'else' incorrectly executes when digit or uppercase expansion occurs, redundantly printing '-'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This 'else' is only bound to the last 'if' statement. It should be part of an 'if-else if-else' chain to correctly handle cases where no expansion condition is met, preventing unintended printing of characters after a successful expansion.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if', so if the first or second 'if' matches but the third doesn't, it incorrectly prints '-' again]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This `else` only pairs with the last `if`, not all three. If any of the first two `if`s match but the third doesn't, it will incorrectly print '-'. Also, when a valid expansion occurs, the code increments `i` but then continues to check subsequent `if`s or the `else`, which can lead to double output or incorrect behavior.]
